helpful: Provisioned petition text and questions-presented extract — enough to classify the legal theory and vehicle without any fetching
Per-Term paid/IFP fee-class grant rates in statpack.json — the right anchor for a paid petition
Snapshot's ProceedingsandOrder — the waiver/distribution/response-requested sequence is the single most informative signal on this docket
gaps: The predict prompt describes a 'Segment base rate by salience band' table in the statpack, but the committed statpack.md/statpack.json carry no such section — anchored on the paid fee-class rate instead
No statpack cut for call-for-response (CFR) petitions; a 'response requested after waiver' bucket would directly price this docket's key signal

No free-text or issue-similarity corpus search: fedcourts query cannot find 'other EUA/PREP Act mandate petitions and their dispositions', which was the decisive prior here (supplied from model knowledge instead)
SCOTUS dockets are absent from the CourtListener RECAP search index, so a companion petition (Curtis v. Inslee) could not be looked up by name
notes: First fedcourts query cost 153 ranged GETs (~40 MB) for 5 rows; fine within budget but worth knowing when planning multiple queries.
